author: codex-agent

patch.value:
Implement the dedicated agent-optimized command family for Plan item lifecycle and step mutations.

Scope:
- Add `pm plan create|show|add-step|update-step|complete-step|block-step|reorder-step|link|approve|materialize|close`.
- All commands must mutate normal Plan items through pm store/history APIs only.
- Every step mutation must append a history entry and preserve deterministic before/after patches.
- Default read output should be brief TOON for agents; JSON must expose stable machine-gating fields.

Harness behavior to mirror:
- Codex: durable living plan with progress, decision log, validation, and restartable context.
- Claude Code: plan before editing, no implementation until approval, task-level create/update/readback semantics.
- Cursor: codebase research, clarifying questions, editable plan steps, file paths/code references, saveable future context.

Command contract details:
- `pm plan create --title ... --scope ... --parent pm-xxxx --related pm-yyyy --from-search "query"` creates a Plan item and records search/duplicate-check evidence.
- `pm plan show <ID> --depth brief|standard|deep --fields ... --json` supports progressive disclosure.
- `pm plan add-step <ID> --title ... --body ... --depends-on pm-xxxx --related pm-yyyy --file path=... --test command=... --doc path=...` appends a stable step id.
- `pm plan update-step <ID> <STEP> --status ... --evidence ... --owner ...` patches one step.
- `pm plan link <ID> --step <STEP> --related pm-xxxx --kind related|blocks|blocked_by|depends_on|discovered_from|implements|verifies` adds step-level context.
- `pm plan approve <ID>` marks the plan approved for execution but does not automatically edit files.
- `pm plan materialize <ID> --steps ... --type Task --parent ...` creates implementation items from plan steps and links them back.

Invariants:
- Exactly one `in_progress` step by default; `--allow-multiple-active` is explicit for parallel agent plans.
- Blocked steps require linked blocked-by item or blocked reason.
- Superseded steps require rationale and optional replacement step/item id.
- Plan close requires implemented/materialized, superseded, canceled, or analysis-only evidence.

Acceptance:
- Agents can use one command family instead of raw metadata surgery for plan steps.
- Commands are deterministic, script-friendly, and covered in SDK contracts.
- Error messages give retry guidance for missing step ids, invalid status transitions, unsafe materialization, and non-Plan item ids.
- Focused unit/integration tests cover command parsing, mutations, history entries, projections, and materialization.
Implement pm plan command family for agent harness workflows
Add dedicated pm plan commands for Plan item creation, step lifecycle updates, dependency linking, approval, materialization, and progressive-disclosure reads.

pm plan command family supports create/show/add-step/update-step/complete-step/block-step/reorder-step/link/approve/materialize/close; each mutation writes history; step links support related/dependency pm ids; outputs are low-token TOON plus deterministic JSON; focused tests cover command contracts and behavior.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Child task created from pm-v7dj. This slice depends on the built-in Plan type/storage foundation in pm-jauk.

message: Create pm plan command implementation slice

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Research evidence: pm plan commands should mirror harness step lifecycle: Codex living plans, Claude TaskCreate/TaskUpdate style updates, and Cursor editable plans with code references. Linked command/contract files and focused regression command for future work.
